description: Many fresh leaves and blades of grass are 'liro'. A painted wall can be 'liro' too. The color alone says nothing about whether the thing is alive or ecologically sound.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and move into high-front 'i'. Begin the second syllable with a tap or short trill for 'r', then round into mid-back 'o'. Stress falls on 'li'.
usage_notes: 'liro lioru' literally describes green life and can describe lush vegetation when the context supports it.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: green color on living and made surfaces
semantic_domains.nature: leaves and grass are common natural examples
